doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_buildopt_gmpinternals_p: build-time predicate reporting whether libmpfr was compiled with access to GMP's internal headers (MPFR_HAVE_GMP_IMPL or WANT_GMP_INTERNALS). C returns int (0 or 1) per a preprocessor branch; TS returns boolean. The TS port does not link GMP at all -- the substrate is pure BigInt with hand-ported mpn_* helpers under src/internal/mpn/ -- so the value is the compile-time constant `false`. The libmpfr-side observable depends on how the system libmpfr was built; the golden_driver emits the TS-expected `false` unconditionally because the TS port does not (and cannot) advertise GMP internals access. Class 'misc' (1000ms per-case budget). Tag minimums (happy>=20, edge>=30, adversarial>=10, fuzz>=50, mined>=5) are inapplicable to no-arg accessor functions whose input domain is empty -- a single happy case is the entire test surface.
divergence_from_c: C returns int (0 or 1) from a preprocessor branch on MPFR_HAVE_GMP_IMPL / WANT_GMP_INTERNALS; TS returns boolean and is unconditionally `false`. Pure-TS does not link GMP -- the substrate is pure BigInt and the mpn_* helpers are ported by hand under src/internal/mpn/ -- so advertising GMP internals access would be a lie. The golden_driver does NOT call libmpfr's mpfr_buildopt_gmpinternals_p() for the expected output (its return depends on the system libmpfr build flags) -- it hard-codes `false` to match the TS-port contract.
refs: mpfr/src/buildopt.c L76-L84 -- the C reference (mpfr_buildopt_gmpinternals_p preprocessor branch).
CLAUDE.md Rule 7 -- tag minimums (carved out here per the spec.doc note above).
CLAUDE.md Rule 12 -- pure-TS port has no GMP link on either Bun or Node; CLAUDE.md Law 3 substrate split.
